design_philosophy.core_loop: Select wrestler → engage in one-on-one wrestling match with opponent AI or human player → pin/submission/count-out victory condition → advance to next challenger
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Real-time grappling and striking exchanges with positioning-based combat; reading opponent timing to execute signature moves and reversals
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Embody a WWF wrestler from the early 90s roster and compete in authentic wrestling matches with signature finishing moves and dramatic moments
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Early wrestling game designed as straightforward 1v1 competitive action rather than simulation; leans into arcade sensibilities with special moves and character differentiation over complex physics modeling. Pre-match interview system was planned but cut, suggesting design intent toward personality-driven presentation

difficulty.continues_system: Tournament/match progression system allowing continuation after losses
difficulty.checkpointing: Match-by-match progression; defeat results in tournament loss rather than game-over state
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Wrestler selection provides variable difficulty through character movesets and stats; appears to support both single-match play and tournament modes

progression.unlock_mechanism: Wrestler selection from WWF roster; progression through tournament bracket by winning consecutive matches
replayability_hooks: Multiple wrestler selection with distinct movesets
Tournament mode requiring chain of victories
Different opponent AI challenges based on wrestler selection

world_building_techniques: Howard Finkel announcer booth introducing wrestlers before matches
Planned pre-match interview system (cut feature) would have added personality narrative
Wrestler roster selection implies WWF canon and character hierarchy

faction_indicators: WWF roster affiliation (face/heel wrestler archetypes from early 90s wrestling landscape)
Wrestler selection creates implicit alliance with that performer's fanbase
danger_signals: Wrestling game with competitive PvP focus; potential for toxic competitive behavior in multiplayer
Wrestler personas may carry cultural baggage from early 90s WWF programming
environmental_storytelling: Wrestling ring setting with announce booth
Wrestler entrance presentation and stat/moveset differentiation
Tournament bracket structure implies escalating stakes

sources_used: tcrf (The Cutting Room Floor - WWF Super WrestleMania SNES article)
